section_title: INTRODUCTION TO EPISODE 8: APPLYING INSTITUTIONAL ORDER FLOW TO FOREX
content:
Welcome back to episode number eight of the 2022 ICT Mentorship, focusing on applying institutional
order flow to forex markets. The session will be short tonight. We will be using TradingView, and for forex
pairs, the recommended data feed is forex.com.

section_title: EURO YEN AND HIGHER TIME FRAME APPLICATION
content:
The daily chart for Euro Yen is displayed. ICT expresses a dislike for Yen pairs, but for completeness, this
demonstrates how to look for specific patterns in forex. There have been many requests regarding the
appropriateness of using this methodology for higher time frame charts, and daily charts are generally
classified as higher time frames.

section_title: IDENTIFYING RELATIVE EQUAL LOWS AND INITIAL MARKET MOVEMENT
content:
Observe the relative equal lows. The market has traded down through them, subsequently creating a run
higher. This part of the analysis will be quick and dirty.

section_title: UNDERSTANDING YEN PAIR BEHAVIOR AND MARKET STRUCTURE SHIFTS
content:
ICT explains his dislike for Yen pairs: they tend to have a 'double return' to a specific level. Observe a swing
high where the market rallies through, showing no immediate fair value gap. Price drops back down, then
runs again, taking out a previous swing high. This demonstrates two points of market structure moving to
the upside, with price trading above them.

section_title: IDENTIFYING FAIR VALUE GAPS AND BUY SIDE LIQUIDITY TARGETS
content:
Within the identified price leg, look for a fair value gap. Two fair value gaps are noted, with the market
trading down into the first one, which then sends it higher. Above this, buy-side liquidity exists, potentially
drawing price to an old high. Similar to playing horseshoes, you aim for the post (liquidity); whether you hit it
or not, the draw is still there.

section_title: DAILY CANDLE TRUST AND FAILURE SWINGS (FEBRUARY 10, 2022)
content:
The daily candle on February 10, 2022, can be trusted to likely go higher because price went up,
consolidated, and then started another run into the high. Whether it trades through the high or not is
irrelevant, because even a failure swing to get above old highs can yield profit if there's enough range from
the open to the high.

Let's examine the specific day, February 10, by dropping down to a 15-minute time frame. We will mark up
the chart, noting midnight New York time. The market rallies into this period, then transitions into the New
York session. A rectangle will highlight the price structure to be analyzed on this 15-minute time frame.

section_title: THE NEW YORK ICT KILLZONE FOR FOREX
content:
The New York ICT Killzone, for forex, is between 7:00 AM and 10:00 AM New York time. This is the crucial
time of day for New York session trades to form. More information on forex time of day is available in other
videos on the channel. It is essential that TradingView is toggled to New York local time; otherwise, the
timing will be incorrect.
FIVE
- MINUTE CHART ANALYSIS:

Within the New York setup time, the market trades down, takes out a swing low, trades below it, and then
punches higher, right above a swing high. We need to determine if there is a fair value gap inside this price
leg. We will break this down from the five-minute chart to the one-minute chart, specifically analyzing the
price leg from the low up to this high.
THREE
- MINUTE CHART ANALYSIS:

On the three-minute chart, confirm if a swing low was taken out (yes, price traded down below it) and if a
swing high was taken out (yes). These are valid conditions to look for a trade. Go back through this price
leg to identify a fair value gap. Two gaps are visible. While price might stab down into the second, aim to
get your entry on the first fair value gap.

When no clear range is present for targeting (e.g., in the middle of a range), how do we anticipate how far
price can trade up or down? In this case, for a long position, where would our targets be if the old high is
further away? While looking to the left on a higher time frame might reveal something, an easier method
exists.

section_title: USING FIBONACCI FOR PRICE TARGETS (STANDARD DEVIATION -1)
content:
To determine targets for expansion moves, take a Fibonacci tool. Anchor it from the swing low that
preceded the run up, then anchor it to the subsequent high after price leaves consolidation, drops to create
a discount, and retraces before running up. Use the standard deviation negative one (-1) level as a target.
Details on ICT's Fibonacci setup are available in the OTE primer video on the YouTube channel. The target
of 133.153 was hit precisely.

section_title: SUMMARY OF MODEL APPLICATION AND PREFERRED TRADING TIMES
content:
Although ICT doesn't prefer trading Yen pairs, this example demonstrates the model's application to forex
markets. The approach can also be used for the London session. For London session trades, mark vertical
lines for 2:00 AM to 5:00 AM New York local time. For New York session trades, hunt for setups between
7:00 AM and 10:00 AM New York local time. Ensure your TradingView time zone is set to New York local
time for accurate analysis, otherwise, it won't work.

section_title: INTERMARKET ANALYSIS FOR FOREX BIAS DETERMINATION
content:
To anticipate market direction, consider intermarket analysis. For example, if the Euro Dollar (or Euro
futures, 6E) is going higher, and the Yen (or Yen futures, 6J) is going lower, it indicates Euro strength and
Yen weakness. This underpinning relationship suggests that the Euro Yen pair would likely go higher. This
is a simple way to determine the bias for exotic crosses and frame the logic for higher or lower price
movement.

Apply the time of day, wait for the taught pattern, and you will not need subscriptions, additional courses, or
anything else. All the necessary information is provided freely on this channel. The methodology taught for
futures indices can be directly applied to forex markets, adjusting for the specific time of day relative to New
York or London. While trades can occur in Asia or London Close, ICT prefers focusing on the New York

- 10 AM NY local time) or London Session (2 AM
- 5 AM NY local time). The approach works,
so enjoy the weekend and be safe.
